cit.basis: Flat 23% corporate income tax (Income Tax Ordinance), administered by the Israel Tax Authority.
cit.note: Preferred / Special Preferred Enterprise regimes under the Encouragement of Capital Investments Law grant reduced rates (16%; 7.5% in development area A). Regime eligibility is fact-specific and out of scope for this SME estimator's flat calculation.

vat.note: VAT raised from 17% to 18% effective 1 January 2025 (approved October 2024).

personal_income_tax.note: Progressive personal income tax up to 47% top marginal rate. The 3% surtax (mas yesef) applies to individuals on income above ~NIS 721,560 (2025), giving a 50% combined top rate. The surtax is a personal income tax, NOT a corporate tax.

social_insurance.note: National Insurance (Bituach Leumi) plus health insurance: employee contributions are tiered up to roughly 12% combined; employer contributions are tiered at 3.55% (lower bracket) and 7.6% (upper bracket). Simplified summary — brackets and ceilings change annually.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Preferred Enterprise regimes, surtax, Bituach Leumi tiers, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
